doc.title: Lessee Finance Lease — Year-End Entries
doc.event: Continuing Ramos Co.'s finance lease: initial lease liability $215,606, first $50,000 payment made at commencement (January 1), 8% implicit rate, and the $215,606 right-of-use asset is amortized straight-line over 5 years. Prepare Ramos's December 31 year-end entries.

lines.why: Interest accrues on the liability balance AFTER the day-one payment: 8% × 165,606.
lines.trap: 8% × 215,606 = 17,248 is wrong — the commencement-day payment already knocked the balance down.

lines.why: Payable because the next cash payment isn't due until January 1.
Straight-line over the 5-year life (ownership transfers, so use the asset's life).
Kieso credits the ROU asset directly; some texts use Accumulated Amortization — either is fine.

memorize.body: Interest = rate × liability carrying balance after any beginning-of-period payment
Finance lessee year-end = TWO entries: interest accrual AND ROU amortization. Students forget one every time.

questions.prompt: Liability $215,606 at commencement, $50,000 paid that day, 8% rate. Year-1 interest expense = ?

distractors.feedback: You applied 8% to the FULL 215,606. The day-one payment dropped the balance to 165,606 before any interest accrued.
That's the payment, not the expense. In a finance lease the payment splits into interest and principal.
You applied the rate to the payment (8% × 50,000). Interest runs on the liability BALANCE.

build.scaffold: Two entries, no cash. Interest = rate × post-payment balance. Amortization = ROU ÷ life.
